transcript.clean_text: stage four of the toronto adriatico and the run to balente ahead of the field snow-capped mountains dotted around the countryside and you could tell that the high ground which will present itself properly on stage six is not that far away well high ground out of the back door today certainly climbing and attempting a breakaway of a substantial force to go up the road two laps three times over the final climb with ramps of 11 were always going to be a nightmare at the end of the day but could it be dream time for the brave we would wait and see once the flag had dropped then it was all hell breaking loose out of the back door a short five kilometer run before some real inclines quinn simmons had broken out of that break away ila cometos diego rosa not happy about the fact that he was stealing mountains points from david de bais who'd failed to make the break and look to be comfortable in that green jersey at least until now well with sutherland restrepo germain carmijan kaithabo rubio chris hamilton diego rosa warren barghe and quintimmons himself up front it was a strong fighting force and in fact they had a gap of around six and a half minutes but that was being paired back ever so slightly by the time we had just over 70 to go it started to fracture some moments out there magnus caught just losing the back wheel taking the left-hand side around a roundabout looked to be perhaps a little bit of oil on the road i don't have some invocation i am certain on his wounds at the end of this evening rough certainly for some but really it was a psychological battle that was about to be played out once we got to the high ground magnus caught back on his bike it'll be a an uncomfortable night for him but really it'll be more of the same tomorrow high grounds high percentages of a high likelihood that this man quinn simmons might be motivated to be in the break again we shall see this time by he wanted mountains points and wasn't that keen on giving anyone else a free ride diego rosa did his best to limit the damage in that king of the mountains competition on behalf of david baies but really quinn simmons was going to be unstoppable so as the kilometers came down and we started to get to the peaks and troughs the chase was being invigorated not by the entire pelican but by a select group that started to think about the chances of maybe going long maybe denying telepocation when we got to those significant ramps of 11 at the end of the day was the breakaway started to fracture and they were being absorbed by the chasing pack the gaps were becoming rather marginal still with the lap to go alla philippe had tried to move earlier on in the day to get remko evan nepal to go long it didn't work because it was marked out by none other than talib catcher himself so they'd had a good preview of the final test and it was only for the brave i think it's fair to say christian rodriguez went early one of many that tried to roll the dice ahead of the final climb well he got to those ramps and was trying his level best to stay clear good driving work by anna damar who just missed out on the sprint yesterday he working for tebow pinel alaphilippe was still doing a job trying to give as much respite as he could to remco evan nepal who'd failed on his mission to go long and now it was going to come down to the final reckoning teleport catcher never looked stressed he'd always been marking out a few either he or his teammates and when the fire was set by evan nepal straight onto his back wheel covered his victor le fay another frenchman ala philippe just off the back and indeed bardet had tried his hand as well and that hadn't worked lander was up here so were plenty of others warren barghe in the frame but pagatcha never that far away from the action bigalanga lopez had also rolled the dice it wasn't to be and richie port had his turn in the end however le faye went for it and pogacchia decided well maybe that was a wheel to follow he did so closed the gap then rounded his man and was to finish off the job nobody could stay with pagacha when he injects pace such as this the sheer belief and of course the talent to back it up is there for everybody to see talibacher heading for bonus seconds trailing heaven appalled by nine at the beginning of the day it was a margin who was to turn around right here super work when you can get it and he gets it more often than not he's 100 so far in races this year winning in the ua and australia yankee what about the torello adriantico well he's in charge took the win today two seconds clear of bingo go nice to see him back after the disappointments of the time trial lafay the brave rebecca evan paul staying in touch but there is only one leader of this race and you know his name danny pagacha who is going to stop him well they will try again over the next three days tomorrow it's the run to fermo and he holds a lead over ever nepal of nine seconds with people ghana on 21. aaronsmen on 36. jagenheart still in the frame very much so bingo reigniting his credentials quinn simmons in green and a more deserved king of the mountains leader you'd be hard pushed to find i suspect he may well try and be in the break again tomorrow w base moving down out of the green jersey into second place ahead of diego rosa picked up points today this is what we're dealing with on stage five it's a bit of a brute and it has 21 ramps at the very end destination firmo it's going to hurt we know that but their suffering is our entertainment the toronto adriatico moves on towards the adriatic
